name: Regression/capability-statement-version
description: CapabilityStatement.fhirVersion conformance ported from fhir-server's Microsoft.Health.Fhir.Shared.Tests.E2E VersionSpecificTests.cs (the shared TestCapabilityStatementFhirVersion helper) together with its per-release partial classes in the R4/R4B/R5/Stu3 Tests.E2E projects (GivenR4Server_.../GivenR4BServer_.../GivenR5Server_.../GivenStu3Server_WhenCapabilityStatementIsRetrieved_ThenCorrectVersionShouldBeReturned). Only the capability-statement-version-check aspect is represented; CreateAllFhirResourcesTests.cs-style per-resource-type creation coverage and the other VersionSpecificTests methods (ObservationDefinition creation, invalid-decimal rejection, new-resource-type creation, renamed-resource-type search) are out of scope for this suite. GET /metadata is fetched once (no fhirVersions restriction) and its result is reused by sourceId across the version-scoped assertions below, avoiding 5 separate HTTP calls. The exact-version checks use the engine's 'http://ignixa.io/testscript/fhirVersions' extension so each one only runs when the conformance run is configured for that FHIR release; on any other configured release it is recorded as skipped, not failed.

test.name: GET /metadata returns a CapabilityStatement with a populated fhirVersion
test.description: GET {base}/metadata must return HTTP 200 with a CapabilityStatement whose fhirVersion element is present. Since the target server's configured FHIR release cannot be hardcoded in a generic suite, the check that fhirVersion is one of the well-known released version literals is informational (warningOnly) rather than a hard requirement.

assert.description: Response body must be a CapabilityStatement
Informational: fhirVersion should be one of the well-known released FHIR version literals (warningOnly: this list is not exhaustive and the target's configured version is not known ahead of time)

test.name: R4: CapabilityStatement.fhirVersion is 4.0.1
test.description: Only runs when this conformance run is configured for FHIR R4 (fhirVersions=4.0). Mirrors GivenR4Server_WhenCapabilityStatementIsRetrieved_ThenCorrectVersionShouldBeReturned, which hard-asserts fhirVersion == '4.0.1'. Reuses the /metadata response fetched above via sourceId.

assert.description: CapabilityStatement.fhirVersion must equal '4.0.1' for an R4 server

test.name: R4B: CapabilityStatement.fhirVersion is 4.3.0
test.description: Only runs when this conformance run is configured for FHIR R4B (fhirVersions=4.3). Mirrors GivenR4BServer_WhenCapabilityStatementIsRetrieved_ThenCorrectVersionShouldBeReturned, which hard-asserts fhirVersion == '4.3.0'. Reuses the /metadata response fetched above via sourceId.

assert.description: CapabilityStatement.fhirVersion must equal '4.3.0' for an R4B server

test.name: R5: CapabilityStatement.fhirVersion is 5.0.0
test.description: Only runs when this conformance run is configured for FHIR R5 (fhirVersions=5.0). Mirrors GivenR5Server_WhenCapabilityStatementIsRetrieved_ThenCorrectVersionShouldBeReturned, which hard-asserts fhirVersion == '5.0.0'. Reuses the /metadata response fetched above via sourceId.

assert.description: CapabilityStatement.fhirVersion must equal '5.0.0' for an R5 server

test.name: STU3: CapabilityStatement.fhirVersion is 3.0.2
test.description: Only runs when this conformance run is configured for FHIR STU3 (fhirVersions=3.0). Mirrors GivenStu3Server_WhenCapabilityStatementIsRetrieved_ThenCorrectVersionShouldBeReturned, which hard-asserts fhirVersion == '3.0.2'. Reuses the /metadata response fetched above via sourceId.

assert.description: CapabilityStatement.fhirVersion must equal '3.0.2' for a STU3 server
